name: METFORAGING found no significant PhenoAge epigenetic-age effect
summary: METFORAGING adds a current human epigenetic-aging pilot with feasibility and safety context but no significant primary biomarker effect.
statement: In METFORAGING, 96 weeks of metformin in non-diabetic older people living with controlled HIV did not significantly change the primary PhenoAge epigenetic-age acceleration endpoint versus placebo, although the study supported feasibility and did not attribute serious adverse events to metformin.
population_or_model: Non-diabetic older people living with well-controlled HIV
quantitative_note: Adjusted between-group difference for PhenoAge EAA was -1.02 years (95% CI -5.30 to 3.26; p=0.627) in the per-protocol population.
caveats: The pilot was underpowered after enrollment closed at 40 participants instead of the pragmatic target of 60.
The population was people living with HIV, not the general older-adult population.
The primary result does not establish epigenetic-age benefit despite favorable point estimate direction.
